meta.note: March 15 evening run — SXSW final day (YouTube creator sessions), RISJ 'liquid content' + AEO + search -40%, Guardian Iran Day 2 analysis, Wildmoka social draft publishing

decisionPanels.title: 🚨 Iran Conflict Day 2+ — Guardian at video velocity, MMR stress test ongoing
decisionPanels.summary: The US-Iran conflict entered its second day with Guardian publishing multiple analysis pieces on March 15: 'Trump wages war on Iran his own way' (David Smith, Mar 14) and Simon Tisdall's 'Humiliating failure looms' op-ed (Mar 15). Guardian continues at full editorial intensity — live blogs, video explainers, news analysis — across a sustained breaking news event. This is no longer a one-night stress test: MMR workflows are being evaluated under multi-day breaking news load. The question has evolved from 'can we publish fast enough?' to 'can we sustain quality vertical output across a 72+ hour crisis event?'
actions: ASSESS: After 48+ hours of conflict coverage, review with producers — are LucidLink ingest and iconik collection workflows holding? Where are the bottlenecks (upload speed, metadata, approvals)?
DEBRIEF: Schedule a post-crisis workflow debrief with multimedia producers once the acute phase ends — this is the most valuable MMR test data you'll get before official go-live.

NOTE: Guardian's editorial framing is now 'human analysis + original journalism' vs commoditised AI news. This is precisely the content Reuters Institute says will command premium subscriptions. MMR's job is to make that journalism faster to produce and distribute, not to generate it.

decisionPanels.title: 📺 YouTube at SXSW 2026 — Creator tools, podcasting, AI storytelling: publisher roadmap signal
decisionPanels.summary: YouTube and Google presented multiple sessions at SXSW 2026 (March 12-15) focused on podcasting, building audiences as new creators, and how AI is changing brand storytelling. This is YouTube's clearest signal yet that it's positioning itself as the primary platform for publisher-as-creator workflows — not just distribution. Combined with YouTube's March 13 live control room vertical update (zero-cost 9:16 from live feeds) and Reuters Institute confirming YouTube as publishers' #1 platform priority in 2026 (+74 net score), the strategic direction is unambiguous. YouTube should be Guardian's primary video infrastructure investment this year — not a secondary outlet.
actions: REVIEW: YouTube SXSW session outputs — specifically the 'building an audience as a new creator' session is directly applicable to Guardian Studios journalists-as-creators mandate.
TEST: YouTube live control room vertical layout on Guardian's next live event (still free, still zero engineering cost — confirmed unchanged since March 13 launch).
ESCALATE: Present the Reuters Institute +74 net score to Guardian's video/digital leadership as the external validation for YouTube-first investment decision.

decisionPanels.title: 💧 RISJ 2026: 'Liquid Content' + AEO + Search -40% — MMR schema must support this NOW
decisionPanels.summary: The Reuters Institute 2026 report introduces three terms that will reshape how Guardian thinks about MMR: (1) 'Liquid content' — journalism structured to reformat and personalise across formats (text → video → short → audio), which means asset metadata must capture format-agnostic structure from creation; (2) AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) — the successor to SEO, optimising content for AI chatbot retrieval rather than search links; and (3) Search traffic -40% over 3 years — publishers forecast >40% drop in Google referral traffic. Combined, these three signals mean Guardian's video-first strategy isn't just about YouTube reach — it's about making content 'liquid' enough to survive the collapse of traditional search distribution. MMR is the infrastructure that either enables or blocks liquid content at Guardian scale.
actions: IMMEDIATE: Review MMR iconik metadata schema — does it capture the fields needed for liquid content? (format tags, reformattability, target-platform fields, transcript/subtitle attachment, original language). If not, add before go-live.

STRATEGIC: Brief Guardian digital leadership on AEO — answer engine optimization is now the successor to search SEO. Video and audio need structured metadata that AI retrieval systems can parse. This is an MMR schema decision.

ADD TO ROADMAP: 'Liquid content packaging' as a Project Launcher output type — one press event → multiple reformatted assets (long-form YouTube + Shorts + audio clip + text summary) as a standard workflow, not an edge case.

decisionPanels.title: 🏭 NAB 2026 (April 18-22): Harmonic + Avid + Backlight — 34 days away
decisionPanels.summary: Harmonic pre-announced the Spectrum X Plus: next-gen media server that dramatically simplifies video ingest/playout, lowers total cost per channel, plus new AI-driven workflow innovations (stand W2831). Avid's new CPO confirmed AI focus — real workflows over big announcements. Backlight at N2829 with 'single platform to manage, create and publish.' Three of Guardian's most relevant vendors presenting in 34 days. The SXSW week has reconfirmed YouTube's creator-first trajectory — NAB will add the production infrastructure side of that story (how do you produce at the scale YouTube now expects?).
actions: Assess whether Ricky or a Guardian colleague can attend NAB 2026 (April 18-22, Las Vegas). Harmonic/Avid/Backlight concentration makes it the highest-value media tech event of the year for MMR context.
Pre-register for Harmonic NAB briefings — Spectrum X Plus ingest/playout cost story is directly relevant to Guardian broadcast infrastructure.
Prepare a targeted question list for Backlight/iconik at NAB: specifically around liquid content metadata fields and any AI-powered reformatting features in their 2026 roadmap.

sections.title: Vendor Watch: iconik / LucidLink / Backlight / Wildmoka

items.title: iconik changelog March 5, 2026: My Shares filtering + favourite saved searches — no new changelog items March 6-15
iconik March 2026 UI overhaul: new navigation, neutral grey background, admin dropdown restructured, Upload button removed — training materials need update before MMR go-live
Backlight 2026 Iconik Media Stats Report — video 64% of storage, audio +45% YoY, vertical +120%, AI used 11.4M times, 903M assets / 324 PB total
Wildmoka supports publishing to social drafts (Facebook/Instagram drafts, YouTube unlisted) — batch social scheduling workflow available now for Guardian social teams without live-publishing risk
LucidLink Connect GA: mount S3 buckets as LucidLink Filespaces without data migration — byte-range streaming, SOC 2 Type II + MPA TPN certified
LucidLink January 2026: Guest Access (secure browser review, zero-knowledge, no install) + Directory Management API (programmatic folder automation) + iOS v3.3.14
NAB Show 2026 (April 18-22): Backlight (N2829), Harmonic Spectrum X Plus (W2831), Avid AI workflow focus — three major vendors in 34 days
Guardian Iran Day 2 (March 15): 'Trump wages war his own way' analysis + Tisdall 'humiliating failure looms' — Guardian at full editorial velocity; sustained conflict coverage output
Guardian Studios confirmed: Leah Green (ex-Channel 4) as head — video-first, personality-led journalism; podcast + vertical video output from launch
Guardian SPUR coalition (BBC, FT, Sky News, Telegraph): technical standards for AI use of publisher content — UK AI copyright delay strengthens early-mover position

Guardian 2024/25 annual report: US audience 30M (+21% YoY), highest US traffic of any UK newspaper brand (Nov 2025 Comscore) — 'ambitious long-term investment plan to become more accessible to global audiences'
SXSW 2026 final day (March 15): YouTube/Google sessions on podcasting, building a creator audience, and AI brand storytelling — YouTube signalling publisher-as-creator as core 2026 strategy
YouTube March 13, 2026: live stream → instant vertical layout in live control room — zero-cost Shorts from any live feed, zero engineering required
Reuters Institute 2026: YouTube main publisher focus (+74 net score, up substantially on 2025); TikTok +56, Instagram +41; publishers deprioritising Facebook (-23) and X (-52)
AWS Elemental Inference: AI real-time vertical video (9:16) from live feeds at 6-10s latency — Fox Sports + NBCUniversal live; live breaking news vertical publishing now viable
Meta March 13: 'Rewarding Original Creators on Facebook' + 'Bringing More International News to Meta AI' — two moves that affect news publisher distribution strategy simultaneously
TikTok 2026 algorithm: watch time > views, rewards niche content, favours authentic human creators — 'qualified views' (>5s) gate expansion; Guardian conflict explainers structurally well-suited

Reuters Institute 2026: 'liquid content' + AEO + search -40% — publishers restructuring journalism to be format-agnostic; AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) replaces SEO; search referral traffic falling >40% over 3 years
Harmonic Spectrum X Plus for NAB 2026: next-gen media server — dramatically simplifies video ingest/playout, lowers total cost per channel, new AI-driven workflow innovations
Avid CPO (TV Tech Feb 2026): AI removing friction without replacing creative control — NAB focus on real newsroom workflows; newsroom AI is Avid's core investment

Agentic AI for newsrooms: 2026 is the year real-world impact arrives — multi-step autonomous agents moving from theory to operations in metadata, compliance, rights, scheduling (NewscastStudio + Reuters Institute)
SXSW 2026 'Who Owns the Truth?': journalist Tara Palmeri + CCDH CEO Imran Ahmed on algorithms, AI, and fractured media — AI governance for journalism is moving into mainstream conference agenda
Digiday: brands pausing ad placements during conflict news — Guardian commercial team should note ad yield around Iran coverage may be depressed despite high editorial value

changes.summary: Tonight's run is steadier than yesterday's — no single explosive event, but the strategic picture is deepening. Guardian continues at maximum editorial intensity through Day 2 of the Iran conflict: analysis pieces, op-eds, and live-updated explainers published today (March 15). SXSW concludes today with YouTube/Google doubling down on publisher-as-creator infrastructure. The Reuters Institute 2026 report is now yielding its most actionable concepts: 'liquid content' (format-agnostic journalism structure) and AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) are now concrete terms MMR infrastructure must support. No new iconik changelog since March 5. No new LucidLink release since January. Wildmoka draft publishing detail added — directly useful for Guardian social team's workflow planning. NAB 2026 now 34 days away.
